title: aquilegia pyrenaica subsp. discolor
intro: Aquilegia pyrenaica subsp. discolor is a perennial plant species within the genus Aquilegia, commonly known as the Pyrenean columbine or Red Columbine. The plant is known for its vibrant red and yellow flowers, which are rich in medicinal properties. The roots and flowers of Aquilegia pyrenaica subsp. discolor have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and digestive issues.
